1779.reason: [This call to parseLVal() consumes tokens without proper rollback on all paths, leading to incorrect lexer state. The method assumes it can safely parse an LVal and then check for '=', but if the LVal parsing advances the lexer beyond what can be restored by setBack(), the parser will be in an inconsistent state.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This call to parseLVal() consumes tokens without proper rollback on all paths, leading to incorrect lexer state. The method assumes it can safely parse an LVal and then check for '=', but if the LVal parsing advances the lexer beyond what can be restored by setBack(), the parser will be in an inconsistent state.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [This consumes tokens without proper rollback mechanism; the subsequent lexer.setBack() only restores to the initial state but doesn't account for intermediate token consumption during parseLVal()]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [This call to parseLVal() consumes tokens from the lexer without proper rollback in all cases. The method sets p=1 to suppress error reporting during lookahead, but if parseLVal() advances the lexer position beyond what can be restored by setBack(), the parser state becomes inconsistent. This leads to incorrect token consumption and subsequent parsing errors.]
